title: D2 auth error codes spec
description: Domain-specialized copy of contracts/error-codes/error-codes.canonical.schema.json, narrowed to the auth catalog: the AUTH_* code-prefix regex, the [401, 503] httpStatus subset, and the 3 auth categories. Conforms to the canonical 7-field set (including factoryShape) but tightens the value constraints so editors catch a non-AUTH_ code, a non-401/503 status, or a non-auth category at edit time. This is the source-of-truth catalog that DcsvIo.D2.Auth.ErrorCodes.SourceGen reads to emit AuthErrorCodes.g.cs + AuthFailures.g.cs into DcsvIo.D2.Auth. A drift-guard test asserts this copy stays field-aligned with the canonical schema.

code.description: Wire-format error code (SCREAMING_SNAKE, AUTH_*-prefixed). Must be unique across the spec.

httpStatus.description: HTTP status mapping. Drives both transport-side response shape AND gRPC StatusCode mapping at the auth-grpc transport binding (401 -> Unauthenticated; 503 -> Unavailable). In the unified generator it selects the base D2Result delegation factory (401 -> Unauthorized; 503 -> ServiceUnavailable). Expanding the matrix requires updating the codegen mapping (D2AEC005 fires on unknown values).

category.description: Closed semantic/telemetry classification. NOT the factory selector — httpStatus drives factory selection. Added to the wire (gRPC trailers + JSON envelope) so consumers can do generic class-based handling without importing the producer's catalog.

userMessageKey.description: Translation key reference rendered as the messages argument on the emitted D2Result factory (e.g. TK.Auth.Errors.UNAUTHORIZED). The generator enforces existence by inverse-transforming the symbol path to the snake_case key (TK.Auth.Errors.UNAUTHORIZED -> auth_errors_UNAUTHORIZED) and asserting membership in contracts/messages/en-US.json (D2ERC002). Coarse on purpose - one key per status bucket so the user-facing surface does not leak which validation step failed.

factoryName.description: PascalCase symbol for the emitted factory method (e.g. BearerMissing, JwksUnavailable). Must be unique across the spec.

factoryShape.description: Closed enum that drives the generated factory's signature variant. standard: the one universal error-factory shape (messages?, inputErrors?, errorCode?, category?, traceId?) — ALL optional; the errorCode / category overrides are how a delegating domain factory stamps a specific code + category on its base status. none: no factory generated for this code. Every auth entry is standard (both 401 Unauthorized and 503 ServiceUnavailable delegate through it); the typed <T> overload on the 503 factories is an httpStatus-driven emitter rule, not a factoryShape value.

doc.description: XML <summary> text rendered on the emitted constant + factory.

deprecated.description: When true, the entry is deprecated: the generated C# constant + factory carry [Obsolete] and the TS constant + factory carry @deprecated. Additive — the entry is NEVER deleted. Deleting a deprecated (or non-deprecated) entry is a breaking change the JSON-diff gate fails.

deprecatedReason.description: Plain dev-facing English explaining why the code is deprecated; rendered into the [Obsolete("...")] message, the @deprecated JSDoc, and the changelog entry. NOT a TK key (developer-facing, not user-facing).

replacedBy.description: Wire code of the successor entry (e.g. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). Must reference a real, non-deprecated entry in the merged registry. Omit for pure retirement with no replacement.

sunset.description: ISO-8601 date for the future RFC 8594 Sunset response header. Edge-runtime-deferred: carried on the contract now, consumed by the Edge response middleware when it exists. Inert until then.
